# Cost and Capacity Planning

Cost is a systems metric. It depends on model, workload, hardware, scheduler,
utilization, and failure behavior.

## Chapter Job

Give the reader a capacity and unit-economics method for inference systems.

## Inputs

- Request rate.
- Prompt length distribution.
- Output length distribution.
- Model mix.
- Concurrency.
- Target SLO.
- Accelerator cost.
- Utilization.
- Retry rate.
- Cache hit rate.
- Quality threshold.

## Outputs

- Cost per request.
- Cost per successful task.
- Required accelerators.
- Burst headroom.
- Failure reserve.
- Per-tenant margin or budget.

## Artifact

A capacity model that converts product traffic into token load, hardware need,
and cost.
